title: Test Layers, Service Containers, Coverage, and Changed-Case Evidence · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Service Container Health Isolation
summary: A green unit suite mocks every boundary, integration tests share mutable state, service containers have no readiness check, and coverage increases while production behavior regresses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci service container health isolation increment in layered test and service evidence plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure service image identity, ports, health, credentials, network scope, per-job state, migrations, fixtures, and deterministic teardown.
Allocate unit, component, integration, contract, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and smoke tests from failure risk and feedback cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example suite mocks every boundary, starts an unready database, shares state across 4 shards, retries flakes, and celebrates coverage while a changed case fails. Design risk-layered, isolated, diagnostic evidence. Competency probe: Configure service image identity, ports, health, credentials, network scope, per-job state, migrations, fixtures, and deterministic teardown. A defensible solution applies Ci Service Container Health Isolation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered test and service evidence plan.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-service-container-health-isolation — Configure service image identity, ports, health, credentials, network scope, per-job state, migrations, fixtures, and deterministic teardown.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-testing-services-coverage-2
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-testing-services-coverage-2
    artifact: test-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked ci testing services coverage 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      layers: [unit, component, integration, contract, e2e, accessibility, security]
      service-image: postgres@sha256:verified
      service-health: readiness-query
      state: per-job-fixture
      shard-proof: complete-and-disjoint
      coverage: branch-changed-line-and-mutation
      retry: disabled-by-default
      reports: immutable-junit-trace-screenshot
    verify: run changed-case tests with fresh service state deterministic seeds report reconciliation and failure preservation
    failure: Reject coverage percentage as assertion quality, start as readiness, shared mutable fixtures, or retry-until-green.
    repair: Select layers by risk, wait for service behavior, isolate state, prove shards, preserve reports, and repair flakes causally.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
